summary.intent: Implement the GridList core component (issue #285) in the ars-ui repository, push it as a ready-for-review PR, and shepherd it through the Codex automated review loop and CI.
summary.outcome: PR #710 was opened on branch impl-285 with the GridList implementation. A CI failure in DOM Browser Tests was identified and fixed by converting two raw integer millisecond arguments to `Duration` in wasm test call sites; the fix was committed and pushed to the same PR.

repo: The repo uses a `waiting-for-codex-review` skill with a polling loop defined in `.agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md`.
Codex signals it has started a review by reacting with 👀 to the PR; duplicate `@codex review` triggers should be suppressed when that reaction is already present.
The `snapshot-reviewed` label must be applied to PRs that include snapshot changes before entering the Codex review loop.
CI includes a `DOM Browser Tests` job that compiles and runs wasm (`wasm32-unknown-unknown`) targets — type mismatches that pass native compilation can still fail there.
A recent API migration moved timing parameters from raw integer milliseconds to `Duration`; all call sites (including test-only ones) must be updated.

code.finding: Two wasm test call sites for `set_timeout` still passed raw integer milliseconds after the API was migrated to accept `Duration`, causing the DOM Browser Tests CI job to fail. Fixed by wrapping the integer literals with `Duration::from_millis()`.

workflow: Passing `labels[]=snapshot-reviewed` to `gh api` requires the key to be quoted (single-quoted in zsh) to prevent glob expansion of `[]`.
`gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{n}/labels -f 'labels[]=<label>'` is the correct form for label application via the GitHub CLI.
CI job logs are accessible via `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/jobs/{job_id}/logs` for targeted diagnosis without leaving the terminal.
`cargo check -p <crate> --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --tests` is a fast local proxy for the DOM Browser Tests CI job.
When a CI failure is found during a Codex review wait loop, it should be fixed immediately rather than waiting for Codex findings, since both must be cleared before merge.

friction: zsh glob expansion on `labels[]` caused the first `gh api` label call to fail silently; required quoting the key.
The `DOM Browser Tests` CI failure was not caught by the native `cargo test` run before push — the wasm target requires an explicit `--target wasm32-unknown-unknown` check.
The Codex review loop required multiple 2-minute sleep-poll cycles with no progress signal, making it difficult to know when to intervene vs. continue waiting.
open_items: Codex review of PR #710 was still in progress at the end of the session; unresolved Codex threads may require follow-up responses or code changes.

`cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject -- grid_list` fails at the package level because filtered runs mark unrelated snapshots as unreferenced — this known issue was documented in the PR body but not resolved.
The clippy CI check was still pending when the session ended; its result is unknown.
